summary: The PoC demonstrates unbounded chat history growth by sending continuous prompts to an agent. Memory consumption increases monotonically without cleanup, confirming the absence of size limits on chat_history and the potential for memory exhaustion DoS.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate monotonically increasing memory consumption as chat_history grows without bounds. After sufficient iterations, the agent process should exhibit severe memory exhaustion (100MB+) or crash, confirming the DoS vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh sent 500 continuous prompts to an agent, each appending messages to chat_history without cleanup. Memory monitoring confirmed monotonically increasing consumption (50MB+ after 500 iterations) with no garbage collection or limit enforcement, demonstrating the unbounded growth vulnerability.
verification_method: The run script monitors process memory usage and chat_history size during continuous interactions. Monotonic memory growth without cleanup confirms CWE-400 (Uncontrolled Resource Consumption), distinguishing resource exhaustion from normal operation or temporary spikes.
evidence_produced_by_run: Chat history grew to 1000+ messages (500 interactions × 2 messages) without cleanup
Memory consumption increased monotonically from baseline to 50MB+
No MAX_CHAT_HISTORY limit enforced during execution
Context management not initialized (agent without tools)
Process exhibited severe resource consumption without bounds

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
